allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps probabilistic-thinking map the interdependencies first so the probability frame reflects the real system rather than isolated variables.
allies.source_quote: When facing highly complex systems (e.g., banking or climate), one must first apply systems thinking to understand the totality and subtle interrelationships of components before creating a probabilistic framework.

allies.rationale_text: Expected-value gives probabilistic-thinking an operational way to compare choices with mixed upside and downside instead of hunting for certainty.
Falsifiability keeps probabilistic-thinking honest by forcing the team to define what evidence would actually resolve the uncertain claim.
allies.source_quote: Strong hypotheses must be framed clearly so they are easier to challenge and pressure test.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias corrupts probabilistic-thinking by treating belief defense as if it were evidence weighting.
antagonists.source_quote: PT, conversely, requires intellectual humility to actively identify and reconcile disconfirming evidence.

antagonists.rationale_text: Representativeness-bias can make probabilistic-thinking judge likelihood by resemblance to a familiar story instead of by the actual odds.
antagonists.source_quote: The conflict is illustrated by the representativeness heuristic, where probabilities are incorrectly judged based on resemblance to a stereotype rather than mathematical likelihood.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Probabilistic-thinking conflicts with narratives when story coherence makes a scenario feel more probable than its base rate warrants, overriding distributional reasoning.
structured_tensions.source_quote: probabilistic-thinking conflicts with narratives (narratives) when story coherence makes a scenario feel more probable than its base rate warrants, overriding distributional reasoning.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Probabilistic-thinking conflicts with simplification when reducing uncertainty to a single point estimate discards the distribution information that probabilistic thinking requires.
structured_tensions.source_quote: probabilistic-thinking conflicts with simplification (simplification) when reducing uncertainty to a single point estimate discards the distribution information that probabilistic thinking requires.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on allies and antagonists rather than on explicit structured tensions, so the file preserves that smaller first-order surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style statistical relation sprawl where the source did not provide an explicit tension block.
open_questions: Game theory and metacognition are source-real allies, but the retained set favors the strongest links into the current curated substrate.
